title: Dan Parsons

{{Quote|The inker takes the penciler's work and finishes it in ink. The pencils may be very rough breakdowns or very tight. When I inked Greg (The Flash) LaRocque's pencils they were very loose. Jan [Duursema's] Star Wars pencils are usually more finished, but it is always up to the inker to interpret rather than trace the lines of the penciler.|Dan Parsons|<ref name="Clone Warz Interview">{{WebCite|url=https://www.starwarz.com/clonewarseu/features/interviews/danparsons.htm|text=DAN PARSONS INTERVIEW|work=The Unofficial Clone Wars Site|author=Brian Gates|archivedate=20040219162109}}</ref>}}
'''Dan Parsons''' is an [[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] comic book inker. Born in [[Wikipedia:Rochester, New York|Rochester, New York]] on [[January 10]], [[1960s#1966|1966]], he grew up with dreams of being an artist.<ref name="ERB profile" /> Parsons became involved with [[Star Wars comic books|''Star Wars'' comics]] at the [[2002]] [[Wikipedia:San Diego Comic-Con|San Diego Comic-Con]] when he met penciler [[Jan Duursema]], who was looking for a new inker.<ref name="Clone Warz Interview" /> In both [[2010]] and [[2015]], he won the SPAMI (Favorite Small Press And Mainstream-Independent cover-dated comic book ink work over another pencil artist {Non-[[Wikipedia:Marvel Comics|Marvel]] or [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]] work}) for his ''[[Star Wars]]'' work at the [[Wikipedia:Inkwell Awards|Inkwell Awards]].<ref name="Inkwell 2010">{{WebCite|url=https://inkwellawards.com/2010-winners/|text=2010 Winners|work=[[Wikipedia:Inkwell Awards|Inkwell Awards]]|archivedate=20250908044927}}</ref><ref name="Inkwell 2015">{{WebCite|url=https://inkwellawards.com/2015-winners/|text=2015 Winners|work=[[Wikipedia:Inkwell Awards|Inkwell Awards]]|archivedate=20250824005232}}</ref>
